analysis.reasoning: Core message: Celebrates International Polar Bear Day, notes threats (poaching, climate change, poor ecology), and credits international and national government programs for helping polar bears survive; mentions 5,000–7,000 live in Russia. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): No mention of the West or moral judgments. RU2 (Russia leads alternative system): No discussion of multipolarity or alternative systems. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner): Implicitly credits government programs (including national and international) aiding conservation, and highlights Russia hosting many polar bears; this frames Russia as contributing positively to a just cause (species conservation). RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not historical. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): No claim of leadership or superiority. RU6 (Russia interesting in culture/nature): References Russia’s wildlife/nature by noting polar bears in Russia, which promotes Russia’s natural heritage. Conclusion: RU3 and RU6 apply.
